name: YouTube Partner Program
payoutModel: Ad revenue share (~45% to creator via AdSense), plus channel memberships, Super Chat, Super Thanks, and Shopping
requirements: 4,000 valid public watch hours in last 12 months OR 10M public Shorts views in last 90 days
Expanded YPP (fan funding only, no ads): 500 subs + 3,000 watch hours or 3M Shorts views
rejectionReasons: Channel content violates monetization policies (reused content, spam)
Not enough original content — significant portion of channel is third-party material
Channel impersonates another creator or brand
Primarily made-for-kids content with limited ad potential
description: YouTube's Partner Program is the gold standard for video monetization — ad revenue, channel memberships, Super Chat, and Shopping all under one roof. The tiered system lets smaller channels unlock fan-funding features at just 500 subscribers. CPM varies wildly by niche: finance and tech earn $10–$30, entertainment may see $2–$5.
quickTip: Your RPM (Revenue Per Mille) matters more than raw views. Finance, investing, and B2B tech content consistently earns 5–10x higher CPM than entertainment — a 100K view video in finance can outpay a 1M view gaming video.

creatorsSay.quote: My tech tutorials with 50K subs earn about $800/month. The RPM is ~$8 which is way better than when I was doing gaming content at $1.50 RPM.
